name: Castell d'Escornalbou and Riudecanyes Reservoir Loop
name_original: Castillo de Escornalbou – circular desde Salou por el embalse de Riudecanyes
description: This moderate 40.8-mile road cycling route from Salou takes you to the historic Castillo de Escornalbou via the scenic Riudecanyes reservoir
region_intro: Road cycling around Salou offers diverse landscapes, transitioning from coastal plains to challenging mountain ranges within a short distance. The region features a mix of flat coastal paths, rural countryside with orchards, and elevated terrain in the Prades Mountains and Serra de Llaberia. Cyclists can experience varied scenery, including reservoirs and historic sites, providing a dynamic environment for road cycling. Best road cycling routes around Salou The most popular road cycling route is Castell d'Escornalbou and Riudecanyes Reservoir Loop, a 40.8 miles (65.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 7 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers varied terrain and passes by the Riudecanyes Reservoir and the historic Castell d'Escornalbou. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Coll d'Alforja – Riudecanyes Reservoir loop from Salou - Port Aventura, a difficult 60.9 miles (98.1 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain, leading through mountainous areas with panoramic views. Local road cyclists also love the View of the Duesaigües Viaduct – Riudecanyes Reservoir loop from Salou - Port Aventura, a 37.9 miles (61.0 km) trail leading through varied landscapes including the Riudecanyes Reservoir, often completed in about 2 hours 57 minutes. Road cycling around Salou is defined by coastal paths, tranquil rural countryside, and challenging mountain ranges like the Prades Mountains. The network offers options for different ability levels, from leisurely rides to routes with over 3000 meters of elevation gain. The routes in Salou are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 5000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Salou's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Salou?
A: Salou offers a wide network of road cycling routes, with over 45 routes available. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy coastal rides to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Salou?
A: Salou benefits from a favorable climate, making it suitable for road cycling for much of the year. The spring and autumn months typically offer pleasant temperatures and less crowded routes, ideal for exploring both coastal and inland areas.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders in Salou?
A: Yes, Salou provides access to several impressive mountain ranges like the Prades Mountains and Serra de Llaberia, offering challenging climbs and breathtaking panoramic views. Routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10461155" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll d'Alforja – Riudecanyes Reservoir loop from Salou - Port Aventura&lt;/a&gt; feature significant elevation gain, perfect for experienced cyclists seeking a demanding ride.

Q: Are there easy or family-friendly road cycling routes in Salou?
A: Absolutely. The Costa Daurada coastline offers scenic and often flat cycling opportunities, with dedicated bike lanes connecting to neighboring municipalities like Cambrils and La Pineda. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38290978" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita Tower of Cambrils – End of the Asphalt loop from Salou - Port Aventura&lt;/a&gt; are classified as easy, making them suitable for families or relaxed rides.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes starting from Salou?
A: Many of the road cycling routes around Salou are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970489483/castell-d-escornalbou-and-riudecanyes-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell d'Escornalbou and Riudecanyes Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a circular route that offers varied terrain and historical sights.

Q: What kind of scenic views or attractions can I expect along the road cycling routes?
A: Road cycling routes around Salou offer a diverse range of scenery. You can transition from bustling coastal towns to tranquil rural countryside with orchards and olive groves, and then ascend into mountainous areas with panoramic views. Many routes also pass by natural features like the Riudecanyes Reservoir and offer glimpses of the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4487615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell dels Teixells&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or notable sites accessible by road bike?
A: Yes, several routes incorporate historical points of interest. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970489483/castell-d-escornalbou-and-riudecanyes-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell d'Escornalbou and Riudecanyes Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; allows you to see the historic Castell d'Escornalbou. Further afield, you can cycle towards Tarragona to explore sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2646467" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tarragona Roman Amphitheatre&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/463399" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Ferreres Aqueduct (Devil's Bridge)&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there parking facilities available for cyclists near the road cycling routes in Salou?
A: Salou is well-equipped to support cyclists, offering dedicated bicycle parking areas throughout the city. Many routes also start from locations with accessible parking, making it convenient to begin your ride.

Q: Can I find bike-friendly cafes or pubs along the road cycling routes?
A: The region's commitment to cycling means you'll find various establishments catering to cyclists. Many hotels and restaurants offer tailored services, and you can often find cafes and pubs in the towns and villages along the routes for a refreshing stop.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Salou?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 70 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, which allow for quick transitions from coastal plains to challenging mountain ranges, and the well-maintained network of routes.

Q: Does Salou offer services for cyclists, such as bike workshops or specialized accommodation?
A: Yes, Salou is a cyclist-friendly destination with dedicated services. You can find workshops for bike repairs, and many certified hotels offer tailored services such as secure bike storage, accommodation packages, and route recommendations to enhance your cycling experience.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling trails near Salou?
A: While many routes are designed for day trips, the extensive network allows for longer rides by combining different sections. The region's diverse terrain, including the Ebro Delta and Montsant Natural Park, provides opportunities for extended exploration, with some routes featuring over 90 km in length, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10461155" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll d'Alforja – Riudecanyes Reservoir loop&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: A beautiful road with wooded sections, which runs wavy along a water reservoir.

The Coll de la Teixeta is an excellent excursion to the Castell Monestir d'Escornalbou - a castle monastery, from where you have a beautiful view of the coastal region. Both the first route, from Duesaigües to l'Argentera, as well as the second one is a narrow, not very busy road. If you want to visit the monastery, you have to take a short spur road with an 18 percent incline.

The Castillo Monasterio de Escornalbou de Riudecanyes is a stately mansion from the beginning of the 20th century formed by the remains of two medieval buildings: the castle, built on the remains of a Roman fortress, and the monastery of Sant Miquel.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty and terrain like for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This is considered a moderate road ride, requiring good fitness. The route covers approximately 65.6 kilometers with an elevation gain of around 710 meters. While most surfaces are well-paved, be prepared for some unpaved segments and steep slopes. The final climb to the Castell Monestir d'Escornalbou involves a short spur road with an 18% incline.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route features several notable highlights. You'll enjoy impressive views of the Riudecanyes Reservoir and the historic Castell Monestir d'Escornalbou and View of the Costa Daurada. The castle, also known as Sant Miquel d'Escornalbou Castle and Monastery, is a significant historical site situated at nearly five hundred meters above sea level.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect while cycling?
faqs.answer: You can expect beautiful and varied scenery. The route offers panoramic views of the coastal region, the Riudecanyes Reservoir, and the natural surroundings of the castle. You'll pass through agricultural areas, follow the Riudecanyes stream, and cycle through woody areas dominated by holm oaks. Many sections are described as very quiet roads.

faqs.question: Where can I park my car to start this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: While the tour starts from Salou, specific parking details are not provided. However, Salou is a well-known tourist destination, so you should be able to find public parking options in the town. Consider looking for parking near the start point in Salou to easily access the route.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty, 65.6 km distance, 710 meters of elevation gain, and segments with steep 18% inclines, this route is generally not recommended for beginners or young families. It requires good fitness and some experience with road cycling.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle in the Salou and Costa Daurada region is typically during the spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) months. During these periods, the weather is usually pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter conditions of winter. This allows for comfortable cycling and clear views.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants along the route?
faqs.answer: While specific establishments are not detailed, the route passes through or near villages such as Riudecanyes and Vilanova d'Escornalbou. These villages are likely to offer local cafes or restaurants where you can stop for refreshments or a meal.

faqs.question: Does this cycling route intersect with any other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other official trails. Notably, it covers sections of the TransCatalunya. De la Mediterrània, [CIMA TA02] Castillo de Escornalbou, [CIMA TA07] Coll de la Teixeta, and Catalunya E26, among others. This means you might encounter signage or other cyclists following these different routes.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Generally, no special permits are required to cycle public roads and trails in Catalonia. However, if you plan to enter the Castell Monestir d'Escornalbou itself, there may be an entrance fee for the monastery or museum, which is separate from cycling the route.

faqs.question: Is the route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for road cycling. While dogs are generally allowed in public spaces, bringing a dog on a 65.6 km racebike route with significant elevation and steep inclines is not practical or safe for most dogs. It's best to leave your canine companions at home for this particular cycling adventure.

faqs.question: Are the roads busy, or can I expect a quiet ride?
faqs.answer: The roads leading to the main points of interest, such as the Castell Monestir d'Escornalbou and the Riudecanyes Reservoir, are often described as narrow and not very busy. This suggests you can generally expect a quiet ride with less traffic, allowing you to enjoy the scenery more.
